title: Context — pqverse
text: pqverse — Monorepo workspace R packages metabarcoding analysis, centred phyloseq object. Named analogy tidyverse. phyloseq object — central S4 data structure (phyloseq R package) grouping OTU/ASV table, taxonomy table, sample metadata, optionally reference sequence set phylogenetic tree. pqverse packages manipulate extend . OTU (Operational Taxonomic Unit) — cluster sequences grouped similarity threshold (usually 97–99%) used proxy species metabarcoding studies. Contrast ASV. ASV (Amplicon Sequence Variant) — exact, denoised representative sequence produced DADA2 similar tools; finer resolution OTU fully reproducible across studies. metabarcoding — High-throughput DNA sequencing taxonomic marker gene (e.g. fungi, 16S bacteria) environmental sample characterise community composition. eDNA (environmental DNA) — DNA extracted directly environmental sample (water, soil, air) without isolating individual organisms. umbrella term covering metabarcoding related amplicon approaches. (Internal Transcribed Spacer) — standard DNA barcode region fungi, located ribosomal RNA genes. ITS1 ITS2 sub-regions amplified primers ITS1-F / ITS2. MiscMetabar — flagship package pqverse, providing miscellaneous helper functions description, transformation, exploration, reproducibility metabarcoding analyses. Published CRAN JOSS. tidypq — pqverse package offering tidyverse-style verbs (filter_samples_pq, mutate_taxa_pq, …) manipulating phyloseq objects four scales: samples, taxa, occurrences, tree. contaminant — Broad umbrella taxon flagged removal represent genuine biological signal sample. Sub-types: technical (chimeras, primer read-), reagent/lab (lab_contaminant), cross-sample (sample_contaminant), artifact. narrow sense (lab/sample/artifact, classified neg_control_classify_pq()) one branch umbrella. detector — contamination-detection method tidypq flags suspect taxa without removing . Pure: returns contam_tbl never mutates phyloseq object; removal separate step (filter seam). contam_tbl — Canonical object returned every tidypq detector: class-tagged tibble one row per flagged taxon, keyed taxon column (matching taxa_names()), plus method column method-specific evidence columns. Row-bindable across detectors, consumed removal seam (filter_contam_pq()) contamination diagnostics. dbpq — pqverse package downloading, formatting, filtering FASTA reference databases used taxonomic assignment (UNITE, Eukaryome, SILVA, …). comparpq — pqverse package comparing alternative representations metabarcoding community, across two axes: alternative taxonomic assignations within one phyloseq (tc_ family — tc_metrics_mock(), tc_heatmap(), tc_circle(), tc_linked_trees()), alternative phyloseq objects (list_phyloseq S7 class + _lpq family). unifying question “≥2 views community differ?”; functions operate single view without comparing (single-pq differential abundance, bubble plots, primer detection, mock-community creation, taxonomy-tree plotting) belong elsewhere. Avoid: treating “single vs multiple objects” boundary — boundary “compares ≥2 views.” view — One representation metabarcoding community compared comparpq. Either assignation view (tax_table rank column, e.g. Genus_SILVA vs Genus_KSGP) object view (phyloseq object held list_phyloseq). comparpq functions must compare ≥2 views; single-view functions scope. taxinfo — pqverse package augmenting phyloseq objects external taxonomy-based information GBIF, Wikipedia, GloBI. augment_tax_table() — taxinfo-internal deep module (merge-back seam) shared tax_*_pq family. Takes phyloseq object per-taxon info_tbl keyed column (info_key, default taxa_name), returns phyloseq tibble’s columns left-joined tax_table. Owns key construction, col_prefix collision handling, left_join, .matrix() |> tax_table() round-trip, rownames <- taxa_names() restore. tax_*_pq function builds info_tbl; external fetch stays caller. taxnames_from_rank() — taxinfo-internal key-builder shared taxonomic_rank_to_taxnames() (query side) augment_tax_table() (merge side). Pastes taxonomic_rank column(s) per taxon single name applies "NA NA" / " NA" cleanup, guaranteeing API query key merge-back join key identical (closing latent silent-drop bug multi-column ranks genus-taxa). resolve_taxa_input() — taxinfo-internal front-matter helper shared tax_*_pq family (counterpart augment_tax_table() merge-back). Validates mutually exclusive physeq / taxnames input, resolves add_to_phyloseq default, extracts taxon names physeq via taxonomic_rank_to_taxnames(); returns list(taxnames, add_to_phyloseq). tax_*_pq function calls instead repeating ~22-line validate--extract block. greenAlgoR — pqverse package estimating carbon footprint R computations, including targets pipelines, based Green Algorithms framework. ggplotpq — pqverse package providing ggplot2-based visualisation functions phyloseq objects. Separates data grouping (fact) colour aesthetics (color_by) faceting (facet_by), unlike MiscMetabar overlap. fact / bifactor — Canonical parameter names across pqverse functions: fact = primary grouping column sample_data(), bifactor = secondary grouping column. Variants modality, variable, var_to_test, .fact unified toward names. merge_sample_by — Canonical parameter name sample-data column used aggregate (merge) samples within phyloseq object. Already consistent across MiscMetabar, comparpq, ggplotpq. pq_to_tidy() — Canonical phyloseq--tidy data-preparation module, exported tidypq. Converts phyloseq object ungrouped tibble columns sample_id, taxon_id, abundance, fact, bifactor, selected taxonomic rank columns (wide, NA→“Unknown”), sample_data columns. Owns full pipeline: verify_pq → clean_pq → pivot_longer → join sample_data + tax_table → optional merge_sample_by aggregation (tibble space) → optional transform (per sample_id, keeps abundance_raw) → resolve fact/bifactor (enforces 2-level bifactor) → optional filter_zero. wrap phyloseq::psmelt() — builds scratch. replicate MiscMetabar::psmelt_samples_pq() (sample-level Hill summary). See ADR 0002. Prefer pq_to_tidy() reinventing pipeline plot/analysis function. _pq suffix — Naming convention exported pqverse functions take phyloseq object primary argument (e.g. alpha_div_pq(), filter_samples_pq()). pqverse meta-package — thin wrapper package (following tidyverse pattern) installs loads pqverse packages single library(pqverse) call. build_all_packages.R — pqverse build engine: runs air format → build_readme → document → test → build_news → build_site → build → codecov package writes CSV metrics issues log pqverse_metrics/. pqverse_metrics/ — Directory workspace root collecting per-run latest-run CSVs build_all_packages.R, build-issue logs, report/ sub-directory PNG figures report.html produced pqverse_build_report.R. merge-release.sh — Workspace-level script merges dev branch main, bumps version DESCRIPTION NEWS.md, pushes. Accepts --bump major|minor|patch (default: minor) --dry-run. ROADMAP_kanban.html — Auto-generated static kanban board ROADMAP.md, served locally scripts/run_kanban.py. Items tagged priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low) facility (easy/moderate/hard). \donttest{} / \dontrun{} — roxygen2 example-wrapping strategies. \donttest{} runs CRAN’s --run-donttest pass (use primary examples); \dontrun{} never runs automatically (use preserve extra examples without contributing check time). Never delete examples — wrap instead.

title: Context — pqverse
text: phyloseq central data structure — Every pqverse package builds phyloseq S4 class rather introducing new class. keeps ecosystem interoperable large existing phyloseq user base avoids cost S4/S7 migration. MiscMetabar scope guard — MiscMetabar stays lean: new features require heavy brand-new dependency dispatched specialist sub-package (comparpq, taxinfo, dbpq, greenAlgoR, tidypq, ggplotpq). pqverse meta-package absorbs growth instead MiscMetabar. Depends vs Imports MiscMetabar — Heavy packages (notably dada2) live Imports, Depends, prevent attached (paying load cost) every CRAN check stage. phyloseq, ggplot2, dplyr remain Depends now; moving Imports deferred 0.17.0 breaking change users. CRAN check-time budget — MiscMetabar archived 2026-05-19 exceeding CRAN’s 10-minute check limit. new examples must wrapped \donttest{} \dontrun{}; data_fungi_mini examples must restricted 5 samples; skip_on_cran() must appear first line test files skip CRAN. Consolidated build/release workflow — pqverse uses two skills (/pqverse-build, /pqverse-fix) plus build_all_packages.R merge-release.sh rather older per-package r-pkg-release / r-pkg-bump-version scripts. latter retained winbuilder/rhub remote pre-CRAN checks. Latest-CSV merge behaviour — build_all_packages.R run subset packages carries forward previous rows omitted packages _latest.csv files, pqverse_build_report.R --latest always renders full picture. per-run timestamped CSVs reflect packages actually built run.

title: How reference databases relate: derivation and coverage
text: Hover node highlight connections; arrows point source database incorporates . Square nodes databases can download dbpq; round nodes upstream (external) sources.
code: library(visNetwork)  dbpq_dbs <- c(   "UNITE", "SILVA", "PR2", "BOLD", "MaarjAM", "Eukaryome",   "Greengenes2", "RDP", "MIDORI2", "Diat.barcode", "KSGP",   "GTDB+", "GTDB_cleaned", "LTPlus" )  all_nodes <- union(c(derivation$from, derivation$to), dbpq_dbs)  nodes <- tibble(id = all_nodes) |>   mutate(     group = if_else(id %in% dbpq_dbs, "dbpq database", "external source"),     label = id   )  edges <- derivation |>   transmute(     from, to,     title = what,     arrows = "to",     color = "#6e2c9b"   )  visNetwork(nodes, edges, height = "560px", width = "100%") |>   visGroups(     groupname = "dbpq database",     shape = "box", color = "#10677c", font = list(color = "white")   ) |>   visGroups(     groupname = "external source",     shape = "dot", color = "#9aa0a6"   ) |>   visEdges(smooth = list(enabled = TRUE, type = "cubicBezier")) |>   visNodes(font = list(size = 18)) |>   visOptions(highlightNearest = list(enabled = TRUE, degree = 1, hover = TRUE)) |>   visHierarchicalLayout(direction = "LR", sortMethod = "directed") |>   visLegend(width = 0.15, position = "right", main = "Node type")

title: How reference databases relate: derivation and coverage
text: information adjacency matrix: filled cell row , column j means “sequences incorporated j”. Reading column tells everything database built ; reading across row tells everywhere database’s sequences end . Annotation-links (taxonomy transferred without sequences) shown lighter shade keep distinct true nestedness.  Three structural facts pop matrix: INSDc (GenBank/EMBL/DDBJ) common root. Almost every database ultimately draws raw sequences INSDc — “independent” means independently curated, never sequence-disjoint source. KSGP sits deepest point. pulls GTDB+, pulls GTDB, PR2 MIDORI2 — three-level chain. Eukaryome, Greengenes2 LTPlus convergence nodes. Several otherwise independent databases flow — Eukaryome merges SILVA, PR2 UNITE, Greengenes2 LTPlus synthesise prokaryote 16S space GTDB SILVA/LTP.
code: lvls <- c(   "INSDc", "GTDB", "Karst 2018", "LTP", "EMP500",   "SILVA", "PR2", "UNITE", "MIDORI2", "EukRef", "EukRibo",   "GTDB_cleaned", "GTDB+",   "Eukaryome", "Greengenes2", "KSGP", "LTPlus",   "MaarjAM", "RDP", "Diat.barcode", "BOLD" )  mat <- bind_rows(   derivation |> transmute(from, to, kind = "sequence incorporation"),   annotation |> transmute(from, to, kind = "annotation only") ) |>   mutate(     from = factor(from, levels = lvls),     to = factor(to, levels = rev(lvls))   )  ggplot(mat, aes(x = from, y = to, fill = kind)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c(       "sequence incorporation" = "#3e135a",       "annotation only" = "#c9b3da"     ),     name = NULL   ) +   labs(     x = "Source (sequences come FROM)",     y = "Target (... are incorporated INTO)",     title = "Derivation adjacency matrix"   ) +   coord_fixed() +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),     panel.grid = element_line(color = "grey92"),     legend.position = "top"   )

text: Nestedness tells databases treat independent evidence. Coverage tells databases interchangeable complementary given target. heatmap maps database clades can assign, annotated marker. Two databases row (clade) alternatives; markers differ complementary.
code: coverage <- tribble(   ~database,       ~clade,            ~marker,    ~role,   "SILVA",         "Bacteria",        "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "Greengenes2",   "Bacteria",        "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "RDP",           "Bacteria",        "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "KSGP",          "Bacteria",        "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "LTPlus",        "Bacteria",        "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "SILVA",         "Archaea",         "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "Greengenes2",   "Archaea",         "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "KSGP",          "Archaea",         "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "LTPlus",        "Archaea",         "SSU 16S",  "primary",   "PR2",           "Protists",        "SSU 18S",  "primary",   "SILVA",         "Protists",        "SSU/LSU",  "primary",   "Eukaryome",     "Protists",        "SSU/ITS/LSU", "primary",   "UNITE",         "Fungi",           "ITS",      "primary",   "SILVA",         "Fungi",           "SSU/LSU",  "primary",   "Eukaryome",     "Fungi",           "SSU/ITS/LSU", "primary",   "BOLD",          "Fungi",           "ITS",      "primary",   "MaarjAM",       "Glomeromycota",   "SSU 18S",  "primary",   "Eukaryome",     "Glomeromycota",   "SSU",      "primary",   "SILVA",         "Viridiplantae",   "SSU/LSU",  "primary",   "Eukaryome",     "Viridiplantae",   "SSU/ITS/LSU", "primary",   "BOLD",          "Viridiplantae",   "matK/rbcL", "primary",   "SILVA",         "Metazoa",         "SSU/LSU",  "primary",   "Eukaryome",     "Metazoa",         "SSU/LSU",  "primary",   "BOLD",          "Metazoa",         "COI",      "primary",   "MIDORI2",       "Metazoa",         "COI/12S/\n16S/Cytb", "primary",   "Diat.barcode",  "Diatoms",         "rbcL",     "primary",   # secondary (contamination-filtering) coverage   "UNITE",         "Viridiplantae",   "ITS",      "secondary",   "UNITE",         "Metazoa",         "ITS",      "secondary",   "PR2",           "Metazoa",         "SSU 18S",  "secondary",   "PR2",           "Fungi",           "SSU 18S",  "secondary",   "PR2",           "Viridiplantae",   "SSU 18S",  "secondary",   "KSGP",          "Protists",        "SSU 18S",  "secondary" )  clade_lvls <- c(   "Bacteria", "Archaea", "Protists", "Fungi", "Glomeromycota",   "Viridiplantae", "Metazoa", "Diatoms" ) db_lvls <- c(   "SILVA", "Greengenes2", "RDP", "KSGP", "LTPlus", "PR2", "UNITE",   "Eukaryome", "BOLD", "MIDORI2", "MaarjAM", "Diat.barcode" )  coverage <- coverage |>   mutate(     clade = factor(clade, levels = rev(clade_lvls)),     database = factor(database, levels = db_lvls)   )  ggplot(coverage, aes(x = database, y = clade)) +   geom_tile(aes(fill = role), color = "white", linewidth = 0.6) +   geom_text(aes(label = marker), size = 2.4, color = "white", lineheight = 0.8) +   scale_fill_manual(     values = c("primary" = "#124444", "secondary" = "#7fa8a8"),     name = "Coverage"   ) +   labs(     x = NULL, y = NULL,     title = "Taxonomic coverage by database (marker annotated)",     subtitle = "Same row = alternatives; different markers = complementary"   ) +   theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +   theme(     axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),     panel.grid = element_blank(),     legend.position = "top"   )

title: How reference databases relate: derivation and coverage
text: Combine two layers designing assignment strategy: Don’t stack nested databases independent evidence. Eukaryome already contains SILVA, PR2 UNITE; running UNITE Eukaryome fungal reads two independent opinions. Likewise KSGP, Greengenes2 LTPlus draw GTDB SILVA/LTP, treat variations one prokaryote 16S resource, independent corroboration. combine genuinely independent, complementary databases. soil eukaryote survey: UNITE (fungal ) + MaarjAM (AMF SSU, clade UNITE -resolves) + PR2 (protist SSU) cover different clades markers independent curation. Pick marker-matched primary database per clade coverage heatmap, use secondary coverage (lighter cells) flag remove -target reads, assign fine rank.

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: dbpq recognizes five taxonomy formats, grouped two categories: prefix-based formats (rank short prefix like k__) positional formats (ranks identified position semicolon-delimited string).

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : UNITE (general FASTA releases) rank identified two-letter prefix followed double underscores, separated semicolons:
code: >AB123456;k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota;c__Sordariomycetes;o__Hypocreales;f__Nectriaceae;g__Fusarium;s__oxysporum ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : UNITE (SINTAX release), PR2 (UTAX format), MIDORI2 (SINTAX format), Eukaryome (SINTAX format) Taxonomy encoded tax= prefix, single-letter rank codes followed colons, separated commas: format used vsearch --sintax original USEARCH UTAX algorithm. Note SINTAX UTAX use header format despite different classification algorithms.
code: >AB123456;tax=d:Eukaryota,k:Fungi,p:Ascomycota,c:Sordariomycetes,o:Hypocreales,f:Nectriaceae,g:Fusarium,s:Fusarium_oxysporum ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : Greengenes2 Similar UNITE format starts d__ (domain) instead k__ (kingdom):
code: >abc123 d__Bacteria;p__Pseudomonadota;c__Gammaproteobacteria;o__Enterobacterales;f__Enterobacteriaceae;g__Escherichia;s__Escherichia coli ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: databases use semicolon-separated taxonomy without prefix. meaning rank determined position string. number levels varies database. formats, use rank_position list_ranks_db() extract specific rank position.

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : SILVA (dada2-formatted), RDP (dada2-formatted) number levels depends specific training set. dada2::assignTaxonomy() classifier accepts number semicolon-separated levels — without prefixes — via taxLevels argument.
code: >Bacteria;Proteobacteria;Gammaproteobacteria;Enterobacterales;Enterobacteriaceae;Escherichia; ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Used : PR2 (dada2 format) PR2 uses 9 taxonomic levels specific protist taxonomy:
code: >EU293891.1.1750_U Eukaryota;Archaeplastida;Chlorophyta;Chlorophyta_X;Mamiellophyceae;Mamiellales;Bathycoccaceae;Ostreococcus;Ostreococcus_tauri ATCGATCG...

title: Taxonomy formats in reference databases
text: Beyond taxonomy encoding, formats differ sequence name (accession number, e.g. AB123456) appears FASTA header: prefix-based formats (UNITE, SINTAX, Greengenes2), sequence name clearly separated taxonomy string. dada2 positional format used SILVA RDP training sets, typically separate accession number — header contains taxonomy, deepest level often serves sequence identifier. distinction matters converting formats: conversion functions like format2sintax() format2dada2() must correctly extract relocate sequence name.

title: Manage FASTA Reference Databases for Taxonomic Assignment
text: reference database metabarcoding taxonomic assignment characterized seven key properties: Name & source — database name, version, official website (e.g., SILVA, UNITE). Tracking exact version used essential reproducibility. Marker — targeted genetic marker region (e.g., SSU/18S, LSU/28S, ITS1/ITS2, 16S, COI). database generally specific one markers primer pairs. Taxonomic focus — clade(s) database provides sufficient coverage enable reliable taxonomic assignment (e.g., Fungi UNITE, protists PR2, arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi MaarjAM). determines groups can assigned confidence. Secondary coverage — Sequences clades outside primary focus, present low numbers. allow reads non-target organisms classified excluded rather misassigned (e.g., plant animal sequences UNITE). representative sequences per secondary group needed; secondary coverage enable fine-grained assignment within groups. Taxonomic format — taxonomy encoded database: Number files (single annotated FASTA vs. separate sequence + taxonomy files) Use guide tree phylogeny-aware classifiers Handling uninformative ranks (e.g., whether unknown_family unclassified_order allowed headers) Header syntax (SINTAX: tax=k:Fungi,p:Ascomycota; dada2: k__Fungi;p__Ascomycota; custom formats) Clustering — Whether sequences grouped reduce redundancy, : Identity-based clustering (e.g., SILVA 99% sequence identity) Biologically meaningful units (e.g., UNITE Species Hypotheses, SH), collapses intraspecific variation preserving species-level resolution Filtering — Pre-processing steps applied download: Taxonomic scope restriction (e.g., keeping target clade reduce database size) Removal non-informative sequences (e.g., entries assigned unknown_order contribute assignment) Sequence trimming selection (e.g., extracting region ITSx, applying quality scores Pintail values SILVA) dbpq provides tools download pre-built databases, convert header formats, apply custom filtering steps address points 5–7.

title: Manage FASTA Reference Databases for Taxonomic Assignment
text: Databases relate two distinct ways: incorporate sequences others (derivation / nestedness), others independently cover clades (parallel use). matrix reads “sequences column database incorporated row database” — every edge verified database’s describing paper. Lighter cells mark annotation-links (taxonomy transferred without sequences). Derivation adjacency matrix dbpq reference databases Two relationships easy get wrong: Eukaryome independent — compiles sequences SILVA, PR2 UNITE — Greengenes2 derives GTDB r207 directly (LTP Karst 2018), sharing Karst 2018 source KSGP. full picture — interactive derivation network, derivation matrix, marker × clade coverage heatmap — article reference databases relate. Independent databases — independently curated, cross-database sequence incorporation (though ultimately draw INSDc/GenBank); use parallel based marker taxonomic scope:

code: # 7a — Restrict to a taxonomic scope filter_db("database.fasta", pattern = "Fungi", output = "database_fungi.fasta")  # 7b — Identify non-informative taxa (e.g. "unknown_order", "unclassified") count_unwanted_tax("database.fasta")  # Keep only sequences with family-level annotation (pattern excludes empty f__ fields) filter_db("database.fasta", pattern = "f__[A-Za-z]", output = "database_with_family.fasta")  # 7c — Trim primers and select amplicon-length sequences cutadapt_rm_primers_db(   "database.fasta",   primer_fw = "GCATCGATGAAGAACGCAGC",   primer_rev = "TCCTCCGCTTATTGATATGC",   output = "database_trimmed.fasta" )

what: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: Runs vsearch --usearch_global match query sequences UNITE reference database, extracts Species Hypothesis (SH) identifier best hit. SH name first |-delimited field UNITE sequence identifiers (e.g. SH123456.09FU). function ports logic nf-core/ampliseq add_sh_to_taxonomy.py script R, using vsearch directly instead requiring external lookup tables.

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
code: add_sh_to_taxonomy(   query_fasta,   unite_db,   vsearchpath = find_vsearch(),   id = 0.97,   maxaccepts = 1,   maxrejects = 32,   nproc = 1,   top_hits_only = TRUE,   keep_temporary_files = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: query_fasta (Character) Path FASTA file containing query sequences (e.g. ASVs OTUs). unite_db (Character) Path UNITE reference FASTA file. file can format (SINTAX, UNITE default, etc.) long sequence identifiers contain SH names first |-delimited field. Files downloaded via download_unite_db() meet requirement. vsearchpath (Character) Path vsearch executable. Defaults find_vsearch(). id (Numeric, default 0.97) Minimum sequence identity threshold (0–1) vsearch --id parameter. maxaccepts (Integer, default 1) Maximum number hits accept per query. Set 0 unlimited hits (useful multiple equally-good matches may different SH names). maxrejects (Integer, default 32) Maximum number rejected hits stopping search query. nproc (Integer, default 1) Number threads vsearch. top_hits_only (Logical, default TRUE) TRUE, report top hit per query (highest identity). FALSE, report accepted hits, useful detecting ambiguous SH assignments. keep_temporary_files (Logical, default FALSE) TRUE, delete temporary blast6 output file parsing. verbose (Logical, default FALSE) Print vsearch progress messages.

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: data.frame columns: query: Query sequence identifier. sh_name: Species Hypothesis name extracted best database hit (e.g. SH123456.09FU), NA hit found. target: Full identifier matched database sequence. pct_id: Percent identity match. aln_len: Alignment length. mismatches: Number mismatches. e_value: E-value match. is_ambiguous: Logical; TRUE multiple hits top identity disagree SH name.

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Annotate query sequences with UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names — add_sh_to_taxonomy
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download a UNITE database first unite_file <- download_unite_db(   version = "10.0",   taxonomic_format = "sintax",   taxon_group = "fungi" )  # Annotate ASVs with SH names sh_res <- add_sh_to_taxonomy(   query_fasta = "asvs.fasta",   unite_db = unite_file,   id = 0.97 ) head(sh_res)  # Check for ambiguous assignments sh_res[sh_res$is_ambiguous, ] } # }

text: default patterns : "^[Nn][Aa][Nn]?$" NaN, nan, NA, na "^[Nn]/[Aa]$" N/, n/"^[Nn]one$" None, none "^$" empty string "^\\s+$" whitespace "[Uu]nclassified" unclassified, Unclassified, xxx_unclassified "[Uu]nknown" unknown, Unknown, xxx_unknown "[Uu]nidentified" unidentified, Unidentified "[Uu]ncultured" uncultured, Uncultured "[Ii]ncertae[_\\s]?[Ss]edis" incertae_sedis, Incertae sedis "^[Mm]etagenome$" metagenome, Metagenome "^[Ee]nvironmental" environmental, Environmental "^[kpcofgs]__$" empty QIIME-style rank prefixes

what: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db
title: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db
text: Runs format, integrity, quality checks one FASTA reference databases returns structured diagnosis: per-file statistics, per-rank taxonomic coverage, table collected warnings, cross-file comparison (flags problems mixed taxonomy format across files), (optionally) diagnostic plots. three axes checked : Format file valid FASTA? taxonomy format detected (detect_tax_format())? taxonomy prefixes consistent across headers? Integrity Can file read completion (truncated gzip fails )? empty sequences duplicated sequence IDs? Quality Sequence-length distribution unusually short sequences, percentage sequences annotated rank, ambiguous (non-ACGT) bases, duplicated sequences, unwanted taxonomic values (count_unwanted_tax()).

title: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db
code: diagnose_db(   files,   tax_format = "auto",   plot = TRUE,   min_length = 200L,   check_duplicates = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db
text: files (Character vector, required) One paths FASTA files (plain gzip). tax_format (Character, default "auto") Taxonomy format assume every file. One "auto", "unite", "sintax", "greengenes2", "pr2", "dada2". "auto" (default) format detected per file headers. plot (Logical, default TRUE) Whether build diagnostic plots. Requires ggplot2 package; installed, $plots NULL message emitted. min_length (Integer, default 200) Sequences shorter counted "short" raise quality warning. check_duplicates (Logical, default TRUE) Whether look duplicated sequences. compares full sequences can slow large databases; set FALSE skip. verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Whether show cli progress bar (ETA) files processed, print summary collected warnings.

title: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db
text: object class "dbpq_diagnosis": list components stats tibble, one row per file, sequence counts, length statistics, problem counts. coverage long tibble per-rank annotation coverage (file, rank, n_annotated, pct_annotated). warnings tibble collected issues (file, check, severity, message); severity one "info", "warning", "error". Cross-file issues file = NA. cross_file list describing agreement across files (formats, format_agreement, n_files). plots list ggplot2 objects (length, coverage), NULL.

title: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Diagnose one or several FASTA reference databases — diagnose_db

Mixed taxonomy formats across files. #>  #> ── Issues: 0 errors, 3 warnings, 1 info  #> ! example_unite.fasta (quality): 5 sequence(s) shorter than 200 bp. #> ! example_sintax.fasta (quality): 3 sequence(s) shorter than 200 bp. #> ℹ example_sintax.fasta (quality): 1 sequence(s) contain ambiguous (non-ACGT) bases (1.471% of all bases). #> ! what: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
text: Downloads reference sequences BOLD Systems (Barcode Life Data) given taxonomic group. Unlike databases, BOLD provide single pre-built reference FASTA — sequences queried taxon via BOLD API.

title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
code: download_bold_db(   dest_dir = ".",   taxon = NULL,   marker = "COI-5P",   tax_format = c("dada2", "sintax", "none"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
text: dest_dir (Character, default ".") Directory save downloaded file. taxon (Character, required) Taxonomic name query (e.g., "Fungi", "Arthropoda", "Mammalia"). marker (Character, default "COI-5P") barcode marker. Common values: "COI-5P", "", "matK", "rbcL". tax_format (Character, default "dada2") Taxonomy format written FASTA headers, file can feed MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). One : "dada2": unprefixed, positional ranks (>Phylum;Class;Order;Family;Genus;Species;). "sintax": >processid;tax=p:Phylum,c:Class,.... "none": raw BOLD sequence FASTA processid|taxon|marker headers (ranked taxonomy). verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Print progress messages.

title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
text: path downloaded file (invisibly).

title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
text: function uses BOLD public API hosted v3.boldsystems.org, remains available main BOLD site's migration v5. tax_format = "none" queries sequence endpoint (FASTA). "dada2"/"sintax" queries combined endpoint (TSV full ranked taxonomy), keeps requested marker, writes taxonomy-headed FASTA (gaps removed). BOLD's taxonomy starts phylum, dada2 output kingdom level. large taxa download may slow hit server limits; use narrower queries, BOLDconnectR package full v5 (BCDM) data model. Please cite: Ratnasingham S & Hebert PDN (2007) BOLD: Barcode Life Data System. Molecular Ecology Notes 7:355-364. doi:10.1111/j.1471-8286.2007.01678.x

title: Download sequences from BOLD Systems — download_bold_db
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Download a Greengenes2 reference database — download_greengenes2_db
text: Downloads Greengenes2 16S rRNA database. default, downloads dada2-formatted training sets Zenodo (maintained Benjamin Callahan). Alternatively, downloads backbone sequences Greengenes2 FTP server. Note Greengenes2 uses d__ (domain) instead k__ (kingdom) first rank prefix. Use tax_format = "greengenes2" summarize_db() list_ranks_db() correct parsing.

title: Download a Greengenes2 reference database — download_greengenes2_db
code: download_greengenes2_db(   dest_dir = ".",   version = "2024.09",   format = c("dada2", "dada2_species", "fasta"),   tax_format = c("dada2", "sintax", "keep"),   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download a Greengenes2 reference database — download_greengenes2_db
text: dest_dir (Character, default ".") Directory save downloaded file. version (Character, default "2024.09") Greengenes2 version YYYY.MM format. format (Character, default "dada2") One : "dada2": dada2-formatted training set Zenodo (recommended dada2::assignTaxonomy()). "dada2_species": species-level training set dada2::assignTaxonomy() (includes species). "fasta": plain FASTA sequences FTP server. tax_format (Character, default "dada2") write taxonomy headers "dada2"/"dada2_species" training set. Greengenes2 trainset ships d__/p__ rank prefixes, dada2::assignTaxonomy() MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq() reject. One : "dada2": strip prefixes unprefixed, positional dada2 (>Bacteria;Pseudomonadota;...;). "sintax": rewrite >ID;tax=d:Bacteria,p:.... "keep": leave original d__-prefixed headers untouched. Ignored format = "fasta". verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Print progress messages.

what: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
text: Downloads KSGP (Karst, Silva, GTDB, PR2) reference database SSU rRNA taxonomic assignment, particularly optimized Archaea communities. KSGP combines near full-length rRNA sequences Karst et al. 2018, re-annotated SILVA prokaryote SSU sequences, cleaned GTDB 16S sequences, PR2 eukaryote 18S sequences, MIDORI2 mitochondrial sequences. Taxonomy based GTDB, providing phylogenetically consistent classification. Also provides access GTDB+ GTDB_cleaned databases built intermediate steps KSGP construction. Three annotation variants available database = "KSGP" file_type = "tax": "sintax" (default): SINTAX-based taxonomic assignments (KSGP Sintax paper). Available versions. "lca": Conservative lowest common ancestor assignments (KSGP LCA). available version "3.1". "ksgp_plus": Similarity-clustered putative taxa (KSGP+). available version "3.1".

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
code: download_ksgp_db(   dest_dir = ".",   database = c("KSGP", "GTDB_plus", "GTDB_cleaned"),   file_type = c("fasta", "tax", "archive"),   annotation = c("lca", "sintax", "ksgp_plus"),   tax_format = c("dada2", "sintax", "none"),   version = "3.1",   verbose = TRUE,   timeout = Inf )

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
text: dest_dir (Character, default ".") Directory save downloaded file. database (Character, default "KSGP") One : "KSGP": Full KSGP SSU database (Archaea + Bacteria + Eukaryota). "GTDB_plus": Cleaned GTDB 16S sequences PR2 MIDORI2. "GTDB_cleaned": Cleaned GTDB 16S sequences (eukaryote supplement). file_type (Character, default "fasta") One : "fasta": FASTA file SSU sequences. "tax": Taxonomy file (.tax) taxonomic annotations. "archive": Complete .tar.gz archive (KSGP files, annotation variants). available database = "KSGP". annotation (Character, default "lca") Taxonomic annotation method. One "lca", "sintax", "ksgp_plus". Used pick matching .tax file file_type = "tax", pick taxonomy merged FASTA headers file_type = "fasta" tax_format != "none". lca annotation broadest sequence coverage default fully-annotated KSGP FASTA. "sintax" available version "1.0". tax_format (Character, default "dada2") file_type = "fasta", also download companion .tax file merge taxonomy FASTA headers (matched sequence ID), file feeds MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). One "dada2", "sintax", "none" (keep accession-headers). Sequences whose ID absent .tax (e.g. SILVA-derived portion) keep accession-headers. Ignored file_type = "tax" / "archive". version (Character, default "3.1") KSGP version. Known versions: "3.1" (2025, recommended) "1.0". verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Print progress messages. timeout (Numeric, default Inf) Timeout seconds HTTP request. default disables R's 60-second timeout multi-hundred-MB multi-GB downloads (KSGP FASTA, v3.1 archive) can complete. Set positive number seconds restore strict timeout.

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
text: file_type = "fasta", function downloads matching KSGP_v<version>.tar.gz archive (one HTTP request) extracts FASTA — , tax_format != "none", chosen .tax file — dest_dir, removes archive. archive roughly 3.5x smaller raw FASTA (e.g. ~686 MB vs ~2.4 GB v3.1), faster lighter server two separate requests. KSGP FASTA taxonomy files otherwise separate downloads. tax_format = "sintax" ("dada2"), taxonomy merged FASTA headers (one sequence ID per row, matched .tax file) .tax file removed, result single FASTA ready VSEARCH/dada2 — original prefix letters .tax preserved SINTAX output (KSGP line starting k__Bacteria; becomes >ID;tax=k:Bacteria,..., d:Bacteria,...). use KSGP taxonomic assignment: VSEARCH SINTAX: download FASTA (file_type = "fasta", tax_format = "sintax"). dada2: download FASTA (file_type = "fasta", tax_format = "dada2"). LotuS2: KSGP database integrated directly. complete set files: use file_type = "archive". KSGP substantially improves Archaea annotation SILVA Greengenes2: Class Order assignments increase 2.7x 4.2x respectively. Please cite: Grant et al. (2025) KSGP 3.1: improved taxonomic annotation Archaea communities using LotuS2, genome taxonomy database RNAseq data. ISME Communications 5(1): ycaf094. doi:10.1093/ismeco/ycaf094

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Download the KSGP or GTDB+ reference database — download_ksgp_db
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download KSGP v3.1 FASTA download_ksgp_db(dest_dir = "databases")  # Download KSGP v3.1 LCA taxonomy file download_ksgp_db(   dest_dir = "databases",   file_type = "tax",   annotation = "lca" )  # Download KSGP+ taxonomy file download_ksgp_db(   dest_dir = "databases",   file_type = "tax",   annotation = "ksgp_plus" )  # Download the complete KSGP archive (all annotation variants) download_ksgp_db(dest_dir = "databases", file_type = "archive")  # Download GTDB+ (cleaned GTDB + PR2 + MIDORI2) download_ksgp_db(dest_dir = "databases", database = "GTDB_plus") } # }

what: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
title: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
text: Downloads LTPlus 16S rRNA gene reference FASTA Bacteria Archaea. LTPlus extends -Species Living Tree Project (LTP) type-strain collection best-quality non-type sequences selected SILVA non-redundant GTDB databases, plus highest-quality 16S sequences deposited NCBI 2019 2025. Sequences clustered non-redundantly 98.7% identity threshold, yielding compact database covers known prokaryotic genealogical diversity.

title: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
code: download_ltplus_db(   dest_dir = ".",   url = "https://biocom.uib.es/opucheck-backend/api/releases/02_26_06",   tax_format = c("dada2", "sintax", "none"),   csv_url = "https://biocom.uib.es/opucheck-backend/api/releases/02_26_02",   to_dna = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download the LTPlus reference database — download_ltplus_db
text: dest_dir (Character, default ".") Directory save downloaded FASTA file. url (Character) Direct download URL LTPlus FASTA. Defaults February 2026 release served Marine Microbiology Group (IMEDEA, UIB-CSIC). Pass different release URL download another version (see Details). tax_format (Character, default "dada2") Taxonomy format write FASTA headers, file can feed MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(). One : "dada2": unprefixed, semicolon-delimited ranks (>Bacteria;Pseudomonadota;...;). "sintax": >ID;tax=d:Bacteria,p:Pseudomonadota,.... "none": keep original accession-headers. Taxonomy read companion CSV (see csv_url). csv_url (Character) URL LTPlus metadata CSV maps sequence accession full taxonomy. Defaults CSV February 2026 release. used tax_format != "none". to_dna (Logical, default TRUE) Convert downloaded RNA FASTA DNA (transcribe U T) rewrite standard FASTA. Set FALSE keep original RNA file unchanged. Requires Biostrings package. verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Print progress messages.

title: Download a PR2 reference database — download_pr2_db
text: PR2 uses 9 taxonomic ranks. "dada2" file keeps nine positional, semicolon-delimited lineage (rank prefixes); pass dada2::assignTaxonomy() (via MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq() method = "dada2") taxLevels: c("Domain", "Supergroup", "Division", "Subdivision", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species") "UTAX"/"sintax" file targets VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX uses 8 standard single-letter rank prefixes (k, d, p, c, o, f, g, s). fit PR2's nine ranks onto , PR2 merges Division Subdivision p: rank (joined -). 9 → 8 mapping : Mind per-method argument calling MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(): method = "dada2" ("dada2" download) keeps 9 ranks — pass 9 names taxLevels (forwarded dada2::assignTaxonomy()). method = "sintax" ("sintax"/"UTAX" download) 8 ranks — pass 8 names taxa_ranks, e.g. c("Domain", "Supergroup", "Division_Subdivision", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"). dada2 taxLevels argument ignored SINTAX path, default 7 ranks used parsing 8-rank output fails. Please cite: Guillou L et al. (2013) Protist Ribosomal Reference database (PR2). Nucleic Acids Research 41:D1108-D1113. doi:10.1093/nar/gks1160

what: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
title: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
text: Detects (uses) input taxonomy format rewrites sequence headers requested output format. primary conversion function; format2sintax(), format2dada2(), format2dada2_species() convenience wrappers around . Supported input formats (prefix-based, detectable rank labels): "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2". Supported output formats: "sintax" — VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX (>ID;tax=k:Kingdom,p:Phylum,...) "unite" — UNITE default (>ID;k__Kingdom;p__Phylum;...) "greengenes2" — Greengenes2 (>ID d__Domain;p__Phylum;...) "dada2" — Unprefixed semicolon-delimited (>Kingdom;Phylum;...;) "dada2_species" — dada2::addSpecies() (>ID Genus Species) Positional formats ("pr2", "dada2") can detected detect_tax_format() used input conversion lack rank labels.

code: format_fasta_db(   fasta_db = NULL,   taxnames = NULL,   output_format = c("sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species"),   input_format = "auto",   output_path = NULL,   id_prefix = "seq" )

title: Convert a FASTA database to a specified taxonomy format — format_fasta_db
text: fasta_db (Character) Path FASTA file (plain gzipped). Mutually exclusive taxnames. taxnames (Character vector) Taxonomy header strings (without leading >). Mutually exclusive fasta_db. output_format (Character) Target format. One "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species". input_format (Character, default "auto") Input format. One "auto" (auto-detect via detect_tax_format()), "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2". positional "dada2" input (taxonomy-headers, sequence ID) assigned ranks position (d,p,c,o,f,g,s); see id_prefix generated labels. output_path (Character) provided fasta_db used, write reformatted FASTA path return DNAStringSet invisibly. id_prefix (Character, default "seq") Prefix used build synthetic sequential sequence IDs (e.g. "seq000001") input formats carry per-sequence identifier ("dada2"). Ignored input already provides IDs.

what: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
title: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
text: Builds taxonomic profile one FASTA reference databases. first runs diagnose_db() (format/integrity/quality checks) adds: richness table bar plot giving number distinct taxa (levels) annotated taxonomic rank, several databases supplied, cross-database comparison taxa present rank, drawn Venn diagram (ggVennDiagram, venn_max databases) UpSet plot (ComplexUpset), one plot per rank. default comparison overlaps sets distinct taxon names rank (presence/absence). weight_by_seqs = TRUE UpSet bars instead show total number sequences backing taxa intersection (summed across databases contain taxon).

title: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
code: profile_db(   files,   tax_format = "auto",   weight_by_seqs = FALSE,   plot = TRUE,   venn_max = 7L,   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
text: files (Character vector, required) One paths FASTA files (plain gzip). tax_format (Character, default "auto") Taxonomy format, passed diagnose_db() used taxon extraction. One "auto", "unite", "sintax", "greengenes2", "pr2", "dada2". weight_by_seqs (Logical, default FALSE) TRUE, cross-database UpSet plot weights intersection number sequences (summed databases containing taxon) rather number taxa. Forces UpSet plot (Venn diagrams weighted) therefore needs ComplexUpset; ggplot2 >= 4.0.0 requires dev version (>= 1.3.6, install remotes::install_github("krassowski/complex-upset")). unavailable, unweighted Venn drawn instead weighted counts still returned comparison$signatures. plot (Logical, default TRUE) Whether build plots. Requires ggplot2; comparison plots additionally require ggVennDiagram /ComplexUpset. venn_max (Integer, default 7) Maximum number databases Venn diagram drawn; beyond UpSet plot used. (Also forced UpSet weight_by_seqs = TRUE.) verbose (Logical, default TRUE) Whether show cli progress bar reading files print summary report. ... arguments passed diagnose_db() (e.g. min_length, check_duplicates).

title: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
text: object class "dbpq_profile": list components diagnosis diagnose_db() result (dbpq_diagnosis). taxa long tibble (file, rank, taxon, n_seqs) every taxon found every rank. richness tibble (file, rank, n_levels, n_seqs_annotated) per-rank taxonomic richness. comparison NULL single file; otherwise list per_db signatures tibbles, per-rank membership data frames, per-rank plots. plots list richness (ggplot) comparison (named list per-rank Venn/UpSet plots, NULL).

title: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Profile the taxonomic content of one or several FASTA databases — profile_db
code: unite <- system.file("extdata", "example_unite.fasta", package = "dbpq") prof <- profile_db(unite, verbose = FALSE) prof$richness #> # A tibble: 7 × 4 #>   file                rank  n_levels n_seqs_annotated #>   <chr>               <chr>    <int>            <int> #> 1 example_unite.fasta c            2                5 #> 2 example_unite.fasta f            3                5 #> 3 example_unite.fasta g            3                5 #> 4 example_unite.fasta k            1                5 #> 5 example_unite.fasta o            3                5 #> 6 example_unite.fasta p            2                5 #> 7 example_unite.fasta s            5                5  # Compare two databases (needs ggVennDiagram / ComplexUpset for the plots) # \donttest{ sintax <- system.file("extdata", "example_sintax.fasta", package = "dbpq") prof2 <- profile_db(c(unite, sintax), verbose = FALSE) prof2$comparison$signatures #> # A tibble: 9 × 5 #>   rank  members                                    n_members n_taxa n_seqs #>   <chr> <chr>                                          <int>  <int>  <int> #> 1 k     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      1      8 #> 2 d     example_sintax.fasta                               1      1      3 #> 3 p     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      2      8 #> 4 c     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      2      8 #> 5 o     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      3      8 #> 6 f     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      3      8 #> 7 g     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      3      8 #> 8 s     example_unite.fasta                                1      2      2 #> 9 s     example_unite.fasta & example_sintax.fasta         2      3      6 # }

title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
text: tax_format (Character) One : "unite": k__/p__/... format used UNITE general FASTA releases. "sintax": d:/k:/p:/... format used VSEARCH SINTAX USEARCH UTAX databases (UNITE SINTAX, PR2 UTAX). Note UNITE SINTAX files use k: (kingdom) first rank include d: (domain). calling summarize_db() UNITE SINTAX file, d: row show 0 sequences — expected. "greengenes2": d__/p__/... format used Greengenes2 (starts domain d__ instead kingdom k__). "pr2": positional format 9 levels specific protist taxonomy: Domain, Supergroup, Division, Subdivision, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species.

title: Get rank information for a taxonomy format — tax_prefixes
text: prefix-based formats: named character vector rank prefixes. positional formats: named integer vector rank positions.

title: dbpq 0.0.0
text: download_ksgp_db() now downloads FASTA (companion .tax tax_format != "none") KSGP_v<version>.tar.gz archive single HTTP request, extracts requested files locally. v3.1 archive ~686 MB compressed vs. ~2.4 GB raw FASTA, transfer ~3.5x smaller avoids 60-second R default previously timed mid-download. archive removed dest_dir extraction; tax_format = "sintax" "dada2", merged FASTA file left dest_dir (extracted .tax removed taxonomy written headers). download_ksgp_db() SINTAX output now preserves original prefix letter KSGP .tax file: line starting k__Bacteria; p__Bacteroidota; ... written >ID;tax=k:Bacteria,p:Bacteroidota,... (previously k collapsed d shared lineage parser, producing d:Bacteria,...). download_ksgp_db() now defaults annotation "lca" ("sintax"). LCA annotation covers much larger fraction FASTA sequences SINTAX .tax (SINTAX .tax annotates ~33% FASTA — SILVA-derived portion left accession-headers). annotation argument also now honoured merging .tax FASTA (file_type = "fasta", tax_format != "none"). Non-KSGP databases (ship sintax .tax) emit warning different annotation requested. download_ksgp_db(tax_format = "sintax") now merges .tax FASTA streaming files awk (via system2("awk", "-f ...", tax, fasta)). R-side per-record lapply previously took 20+ minutes 2.4 GB / 1.77 M-record KSGP v3.1 FASTA replaced single shell pass completes seconds. output form (>ID;tax=k:Bacteria,p:Bacteroidota,c:Bacteroidia,..., VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX-compatible) identical previous R pipeline. __ compartment tag used KSGP organelles (e.g. Eukaryota__mito) now converted : value (canonical SINTAX form, still parseable VSEARCH). tax_format values ("dada2", "dada2_species", "unite", "greengenes2") keep slower R-based merge since used KSGP. download_ksgp_db() (internal download_file() helper used every download_*_db()) gain timeout argument, default Inf, multi-GB downloads longer hit R’s 60-second options("timeout") ceiling. Set timeout = 600 (value seconds) restore strict deadline. Added comprehensive test suite (tests/testthat/test-format-databases.R) format- summarize-related functions, covering supported taxonomy header formats (UNITE, SINTAX, Greengenes2, dada2, dada2_species, PR2) plus deliberately erroneous fixtures (empty file, short sequences, duplicated IDs, duplicated sequences, ambiguous bases, unrecognized format, inconsistent ranks, unwanted taxonomic values, gzipped input). Download functions now produce FASTA files taxonomy headers, ready MiscMetabar::add_new_taxonomy_pq(), via tax_format argument ("dada2"/"sintax"). download_greengenes2_db() strips d__/p__ prefixes plain dada2 (prefixed form rejected assignTaxonomy()); download_ksgp_db() merges companion .tax headers sequence ID; download_ltplus_db() merges taxonomy CSV; download_marjaam_db() uses QIIME release (FASTA + taxonomy table); download_bold_db() pulls full ranked taxonomy BOLD’s combined endpoint. download_marjaam_db() now downloads current MaarjAM QIIME release (dataset argument selects "SSU" (default), "SSU_TYPE", "LSU", "full_ITS" "onlyITS"); previous default URL stopped working. download_bold_db() queries still-available v3.boldsystems.org API HTTPS , default, returns sequences requested marker full ranked-taxonomy headers (BOLD’s main site migrated v5; combined endpoint remains accessible). New article reference databases relate documents sequence-derivation (nestedness) taxonomic-coverage relationships supported databases interactive network two adjacency/coverage matrices, every derivation edge sourced describing paper. Corrects README diagram: EUKARYOME incorporates SILVA, PR2 UNITE (independent), Greengenes2 derives GTDB r207 directly (sharing Karst 2018 source KSGP). New function download_ltplus_db() downloads LTPlus 16S rRNA reference FASTA Bacteria Archaea, extends -Species Living Tree Project (LTP) best-quality non-type sequences SILVA, GTDB NCBI (98.7% identity clustering). (unaligned) FASTA fetched directly LTP release server , default (to_dna = TRUE), transcribed RNA clean DNA FASTA. default (tax_format = "dada2") also merges companion taxonomy CSV headers (100% sequences annotated), file feeds dada2/VSEARCH add_new_taxonomy_pq() directly; url parameter selects releases (Rosselló-Móra et al. 2026, ). New function download_ksgp_db() downloads KSGP (Karst, Silva, GTDB, PR2) SSU reference database GTDB+ GTDB_cleaned components. Supports three annotation variants ("sintax", "lca", "ksgp_plus") complete archive download. KSGP optimized Archaea taxonomic assignment, improving Class Order assignments 2.7x 4.2x SILVA (Grant et al. 2025, ). download_silva_db() gains format = "sintax", downloads official arb-silva DADA2 toSpecies trainset converts locally VSEARCH/USEARCH SINTAX database (7 ranks d,p,c,o,f,g,s, written *_sintax.fasta.gz). Synthetic sequence labels (SILVA<version>_<target>_NNNNNN) generated dada2 trainset carries accession. download_silva_db() now sources dada2-formatted files official arb-silva DADA2 release instead Zenodo, supports target = "LSU" dada2, dada2_species, sintax formats (previously SSU ). format_fasta_db() format2sintax() accept input_format = "dada2" (positional, prefix-less taxonomy) gain id_prefix argument label records carry sequence ID. detect_tax_format() now recognises positional dada2 headers (returns "dada2") instead falling "unknown". New function diagnose_db() runs format, integrity, quality checks one several FASTA reference databases returns structured dbpq_diagnosis object: per-file statistics, per-rank annotation coverage, tibble collected issues (info/warning/error severities), cross-file comparison flags mixed taxonomy format, optional ggplot2 diagnostic plots. detects empty short sequences, duplicated IDs sequences, ambiguous (non-ACGT) bases, unwanted taxonomic values, unreadable truncated files. verbose = TRUE (default) shows cli progress bar per-file ETA colour-coded summary collected issues. New function add_sh_to_taxonomy() (marked experimental) annotates query sequences UNITE Species Hypothesis (SH) names running vsearch --usearch_global UNITE reference database extracting SH identifiers matched sequence headers. Detects ambiguous assignments multiple top hits disagree SH name. Ports logic nf-core/ampliseq add_sh_to_taxonomy.py script R. count_pattern_db() count_seq_db() longer emit spurious warning pattern zero matches (example counting sequences empty file). count_pattern_db() filter_db() now quote file paths search patterns passed shell commands, paths patterns containing spaces shell metacharacters handled correctly. New function find_vsearch() locates vsearch executable system PATH verifies user-supplied path. New function is_vsearch_installed() checks whether vsearch available system. list_ranks_db() now emits informative message taxa match requested rank prefix, suggesting detect_tax_format() identify file’s taxonomy format. New function profile_db() profiles taxonomic content one several databases: runs diagnose_db() adds per-rank richness table bar plot (number distinct taxa, “levels”, rank) , multiple databases, per-rank cross-database comparison taxa present, drawn Venn diagram (ggVennDiagram, venn_max databases) UpSet plot (ComplexUpset). weight_by_seqs = TRUE UpSet intersections weighted number sequences instead number taxa; ggplot2 >= 4.0.0 needs dev ComplexUpset (>= 1.3.6), otherwise unweighted Venn drawn weighted counts remain available comparison$signatures. summarize_db() now handles empty FASTA databases gracefully, reporting zero sequences instead emitting warnings Inf length statistics.

title: dbpq 0.1
text: Initial development version. cutadapt_rm_primers_db() now checks exit code cutadapt stops informative error binary missing fails, instead silently continuing. download_unite_db() now emits message type = "static" taxon_group = "fungi" clarify UNITE v10.0 ship separate static/dynamic archives fungi. filter_db() documentation now runnable example using bundled example_unite.fasta file. get_file_extension() longer emits spurious warning double-extension files (e.g. .fasta.gz), standard format compressed databases. example FASTA file (inst/extdata/example_unite.fasta, 5 sequences UNITE format) now bundled package, enabling runnable examples count_seq_db(), count_pattern_db(), detect_tax_format(), filter_db(), list_ranks_db(), summarize_db(). tax_prefixes("sintax") documentation now clarifies UNITE SINTAX files use k: (kingdom) first rank include d: (domain) level; d: 0 sequences row summarize_db() output therefore expected. detect_tax_format() auto-detects taxonomy format ("default", "sintax", "greengenes2") FASTA headers. download_diatbarcode_db() downloads Diat.barcode rbcL reference database diatoms. download_eukaryome_db() now lists SINTAX format download page (https://eukaryome.org/sintax/) instructions. download_greengenes2_db() downloads Greengenes2 16S rRNA database (dada2 format Zenodo plain FASTA FTP). download_midori2_db() downloads MIDORI2 mitochondrial reference database COI markers. download_pr2_db() now accepts format = "sintax" alias "UTAX". Documentation now mentions pr2database R package complementary tool. download_rdp_db() downloads RDP 16S rRNA database (dada2-formatted training sets Zenodo). download_unite_db() gains taxonomic_format parameter ("default" "sintax") download SINTAX-formatted FASTA files directly, ready use vsearch --sintax. list_ranks_db() summarize_db() gain tax_format parameter handle different taxonomy header formats ("unite", "sintax", "greengenes2", "pr2", "auto"). list_ranks_db() also gains rank_position parameter positional (prefix-less) taxonomy headers. tax_prefixes() returns rank prefixes given taxonomy format, use list_ranks_db() summarize_db(). count_unwanted_tax() scans taxonomy table (phyloseq object FASTA reference database) common problematic values "unclassified", "unknown", "Incertae_sedis", NA-like strings, empty QIIME-style rank prefixes. Returns tibble summarising matches per pattern rank. Suggests MiscMetabar::verify_tax_table() cleaning input phyloseq object. Default patterns sourced MiscMetabar::unwanted_tax_patterns MiscMetabar installed, built-fallback otherwise. count_pattern_db() counts sequences matching pattern FASTA files. count_seq_db() counts total sequences FASTA file. filter_db() filters FASTA database taxonomic pattern. format_fasta_db() new unified function convert FASTA taxonomy headers supported format ("sintax", "unite", "greengenes2", "dada2", "dada2_species"), auto-detection input format. format2dada2(), format2dada2_species(), format2sintax() now accept input_format argument ("auto", "sintax", "unite", "greengenes2") instead from_sintax, support Greengenes2 additional input format. now wrappers around format_fasta_db(). cutadapt_rm_primers_db() removes primer sequences FASTA database using cutadapt.
